title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: Three Statements are given followed by four conclusions numbered 1, 2, 3, and 4. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically does NOT follow(s) from the statements. Statements: I. All Fruits are Green. II. Some Green are Vegetable. III. Some Green are Polybags. Conclusions: 1) Some Vegetable are Polybags. 2) Some Green are Fruits. 3) All Fruits are polybags 4) Some Vegetables are Polybags. ​​
questions.explanation: Given: Statements: I. All Fruits are Green. II. Some Green are Vegetable. III. Some Green are Polybags. From the given statements Venn diagram will be: Conclusions: 1) Some Vegetable are Polybags → Does not follow. There is no information about a direct connection between Vegetables and Polybags. 2) Some Green are Fruits → Follows. All Fruits are Green. So, this is a standard inference. 3) All Fruits are polybags → Does not follow. All Fruits are Green. Some Green are Polybags. But again, this doesn’t mean all Fruits (which are part of Green) are Polybags. 4) Some Vegetables are Polybags → Does not follow. No direct link between Vegetables and Polybags. So, Only 1, 3 and 4 do not follow. Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: 1. Authority 2. Automate 3. Authentic 4. Auspicious 5. Aureole
questions.explanation: 5. Aureole 4. Auspicious 3. Authentic 1. Authority 2. Automate So, the correct order is: '5, 4, 3, 1, 2'.

questions.question: What will come at the place of question mark (?) 1, 2, 6, 21, 88, ?
questions.explanation: Given: 1, 2, 6, 21, 88, ? Logic: Numbers are multiply by natural number and add natural number from 1. 1 × 1 + 1 = 2 2 × 2 + 2 = 6 6 × 3 + 3 = 21 21 × 4 + 4 = 88 88 × 5 + 5 = 445 So, the missing term is 445. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: How many letters will remain unchanged if the word MOBILE is arranged alphabetically?
questions.explanation: Given: MOBILE Given Letters M O B I L E Alphabetical Order B E I L M O Transformed word: BEILMO So, 0 letters will remain unchanged if the word is arranged alphabetically. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Complete the series: a a a _ c c b b _ a a a c c c _ b b a a a c c _ b b b​​
questions.explanation: ​Given: a a a _ c c b b _ a a a c c c _ b b a a a c c _ b b b​ Now, we check each options. ​Option (a): b b c c a a a / b c c / b b b / a a a / c c c / c b b / a a a / c c c / b b b​ ​Option (b): c b b c a a a / c c c / b b b / a a a / c c c / b b b / a a a / c c c / b b b ​(It is a logical repeating pattern.)​ ​Option (c): b c b c a a a / b c c / b b c / a a a / c c c / b b b / a a a / c c c / b b b​ ​Option (d): c c b b a a a / c c c / b b c / a a a / c c c / b b b / a a a / c c b / b b b​ So, the logical pattern is: a a a / c c c / b b b Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Refer to the given letter series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) Y M C K R X Z S P O L I H J F V E N U B W G Q (Right) How many such consonants are there each of which is immediately preceded by a vowel and also immediately followed by a vowel?
questions.explanation: Given series: Y M C K R X Z S P O L I H J F V E N U B W G Q Logic: Count consonants that have a vowel before and a vowel after them. (O _ L _ I) → L has vowels on both sides (E _ N _ U) → N has vowels on both sides Only L and N meet the condition. So the answer is 2. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting around a square table facing the centre of the table. Some of them are sitting at the corners, while some are sitting at the exact centre of the sides. E sits third to the right of B. B sits at one of the corners. G sits second to the right of E. Only one person sits between G and F. A sits to the immediate right of H. Only three people sit between A and D. Who sits to the immediate right of C?
questions.explanation: Given - A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting around a square table facing the centre of the table. Some of them are sitting at the corners, while some are sitting at the exact centre of the sides. E sits third to the right of B. B sits at one of the corners. G sits second to the right of E. Only one person sits between G and F. A sits to the immediate right of H. Only three people sit between A and D. G sits to the immediate right of C.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term on the same basis as the second term is related to the first term. 12 : 30 :: 20 : ?
questions.explanation: Given: 12 : 30 :: 20 : ? Logic: The number is written as a product of two consecutive numbers, and both numbers increase by 2. Explanation: 12 = 3 × 4 30 = 5 × 6 So the pattern is: (3, 4) → (5, 6) Now apply the same logic: 20 = 4 × 5 Next pair = 6 × 7 = 42 Thus, correct option is (B).

questions.question: Complete the series: x x _ y z z _ x y y z _ x x y y _ z
questions.explanation: Given: x x _ y z z _ x y y z _ x x y y _ z Now, we check each options. Option (a): y x z z x x y y z z / x x y y z z / x x y y z z ​(It is a logical repeating pattern.)​​ Option (b): y x x z x x y y z z / x x y y z x / x x y y z z Option (c): z y x x x x z y z z / y x y y z x / x x y y x z​ Option (d): y z x x x x y y z z / z x y y z x / x x y y x z​ So, the following pattern is: x x y y z z Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 3485769 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. What will be the sum of the digits which are fifth from the left and fourth from the right in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: ​Given: 3485769 ​Logic: Digits of 3485769 arranged in ascending order → 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 5th from the left = 7 4th from the right = 6 Sum = 7 + 6 = 13 Thus, the correct option is (A) 13.​

questions.question: In the following question, the ﬁrst and second numbers are related in some way, choose the alternative which is similarly related to the third number 152 : 1064 :: 133 : ?
questions.explanation: Given: 152 : 1064 :: 133 : ? Logic: 1st number ×\times× 7 = 2nd number For, 152 : 1064 152 ×\times×​ 7 = 1064 Similarly, 133 : ? 133 ×\times× 7 = 931 So, 133 : 931 Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Out of the given options, three are similar in a certain manner. However, one option is NOT like the other three. Select the option which is different from the rest.
questions.explanation: A) WSXZ W −2→ U (but third letter is X) S +5→ X (but fourth letter is Z) Does not follow the pattern B) NJLO N −2→ L J +5→ O Follows C) SOQT S −2→ Q O +5→ T Follows D) HDFI H −2→ F D +5→ I Follows Three options follow the same logic, but A does not. Thus, correct option is (A).

questions.question: Kavita starts from Point A and drives 7 km towards east. She then takes a left turn and drives 12 km. She takes a final left turn; drives 10 km and stops at Point P. If a pole is located 12 km south of Point P, how far and in which direction would the pole be from Kavita’s starting point? (All the turns are 90° turns only, unless specified.)
questions.explanation: Given - Kavita starts from Point A and drives 7 km towards east. She then takes a left turn and drives 12 km. She takes a final left turn; drives 10 km and stops at Point P. If a pole is located 12 km south of Point P, how far =10 - 7 = 3 km and in which direction would the pole be from Kavita’s starting point = West

questions.question: Three different positions of the same dice are shown. Find the number on the face opposite the face having '1' ​
questions.explanation: If two dice have the same face value in the given image then their clockwise or anticlockwise wise number are known as opposite numbers in dice. opposite of 1 is : So, opposite of 1 is 3 Thus, the correct option is:

questions.question: Statement: All engineers are graduates. Rohit is an engineer. What follows?
questions.explanation: ​Statement: All engineers are graduates. Rohit is an engineer. Now, we check each options. Option (a): Rohit is experienced. Cannot be concluded — experience is not mentioned. ​Option (b): Rohit is a graduate. Correct — if all engineers are graduates and Rohit is an engineer, then Rohit must be a graduate. ​Option (c): Rohit is a manager. Cannot be concluded — wob role is not given. ​Option (d): Rohit is unemployed. Cannot be concluded — no information supports this. So, Rohit is a graduate is follows. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘YJM’ is coded as ’26-11-14’ and ‘SOP’ is coded as ’20-16-17’. What is the code for ‘BTX’ in the given code language?
questions.explanation: Given: ‘YJM’ → ’26-11-14’ ‘SOP’ → ’20-16-17’ Question: What is the code for ‘BTX’? Logic: = Each letter is converted to (Positional value + 1) Step 1: Find the positional value of each letter. Y = 25, J = 10, M = 13 Observation: Each letter’s position is increased by 1.Y (25) + 1 → 26 J (10) + 1 → 11 M (13) + 1 → 14 Similarly: S = 19, O = 15, P = 16 Code: 20-16-17 Explanation: Apply the same rule to BTX: B = 2 → 2 + 1 = 3 T = 20 → 20 + 1 = 21 X = 24 → 24 + 1 = 25 So, the code for BTX is: 3-21-25 Final Answer: 3-21-25 Correct Option: A Alternative method; hence option a is correct.

questions.question: Find the odd one out.
questions.explanation: Logic: 1st number​2= 2nd number Now, we check each options. Option (a): 4 : 16 42 = 16 Option (b): 5 : 25 52 = 25 Option (c): 6 : 36 62 = 36 Option (d): 8 : 54 82 = 64 ≠\ne=​ 54 So, 8 : 54 is odd one out. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Find the odd one:
questions.explanation: Solution: Analyze the shapes: 1. Rectangle: 2D figure (Plane geometry) 2. Square: 2D figure (Plane geometry) 3. Triangle: 2D figure (Plane geometry) 4. Cube: 3D figure (Solid geometry) The Cube is the only three-dimensional obwect in the list. So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: If # = +, @ = ×, % = -, then find the value of: (6 @ 3) % 4 # 5?
questions.explanation: Given: (6 @ 3) % 4 # 5? Given Symbol # @ % New Sign + × - Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ New equation: (6 × 3) - 4 + 5 = ? 18 - 4 + 5 = ? 23 - 4 = ? ? = 19 Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: If LIGHT is coded as KJFIS, then HEAVY is coded as?
questions.explanation: Given: If LIGHT is coded as KJFIS. Logic: Letters are - 1 and + 1 place alternately. For, LIGHT - KJFIS L - 1 = K, I + 1 = J, G - 1 = F, H + 1 = I, T - 1 = S Similary, HEAVY - ? H - 1 = G, E + 1 = F, A - 1 = Z, V + 1 = W, Y - 1 = X So, HEAVY is coded as GFZWX. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding to/subtracting from/multiplying with 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) (22, 28, 31) (39, 45, 48)
questions.explanation: Given: (22, 28, 31) and (39, 45, 48) Logic: 2nd number = 1st number + 6 3rd number = 2nd number + 3 Set (22, 28, 31) 22 + 6 = 28 28 + 3 = 31 Set (39, 45, 48) 39 + 6 = 45 45 + 3 = 48 Now check each option: Option (A) (53, 59, 65) 53 + 6 = 59, 59 + 3 = 62 (≠ 65) → Not follow Option (B) (44, 47, 50) 44 + 6 = 50 (≠ 47) → Not follow Option (C) (17, 20, 26) 17 + 6 = 23 (≠ 20) → Not follow Option (D) (27, 33, 36) 27 + 6 = 33, 33 + 3 = 36 → Pattern follow Thus, the correct option is (D) (27, 33, 36).

questions.question: If + is ×, × is -, - is ÷ and ÷ is +, then 6 + 4 × 24 - 8 ÷ 4 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: 6 + 4 × 24 - 8 ÷ 4 = ? Given Sign + × - ÷ New Sign × - ÷ + Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ New equation: ​​6 × 4 - 24 ÷ 8 + 4 = ? 6 × 4 - 3 + 4 = ? 24 - 3 + 4 = ? 28 - 3 = ? ? = 25 Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which of the following is/are identical to the address given: Priya Yadav B-22, Rosewood Heights, Sector 15, Gurgaon, 122002 1. Priya Yadav B-22, Rosewood Heights, Sector 15, Gurgaon, 122002 2. Priya Yadav B-22, Rosewood Heights, Sector 15, Gurugram, 122002 3. Priya Yadav B-22, Rosewood Heights, Sector 15, Gurgaon, 122003 4. Priya Yadav B-22, Rosewood Heights, Sector 15, Gurgaon, 122001
questions.explanation: Name: Priya Yadav House No.: B-22 Locality: Rosewood Heights Sector: 15 City: Gurgaon PIN Code: 122002 Explanation: Check each statement: Statement 1: All details exactly match the given address ✔ Statement 2: Gurgaon ≠ Gurugram (different city name) ✘ Statement 3: PIN code 122003 ≠ 122002 ✘ Statement 4: PIN code 122001 ≠ 122002 ✘ Only Statement 1 is completely identical.

questions.question: In a certain code language, A × B means ‘A is the wife of B’ A + B means ‘A is the brother of B’ A ? B means ‘A is the father of B’ A ~ B means ‘A is the daughter of B’ Based on the above, how is E related to Y if E ~ N ? J + O × Y = ?
questions.explanation: Given: A × B means ‘A is the wife of B’ If E ~ N ? J + O × Y? Symbols ​×\times×​​ + ? ~ Relation Wife Brother Father Daughter From the given information blood relation diagram will be. E is Wife's sister of Y. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: How many maximum number of triangles are there in the given question figure?
questions.explanation: Given: There are 30 triangles in this diagram.
